steps.title: Write the sum over even values
steps.text: For X ~ Poisson(λ), collect all even non-negative integers:

steps.title: Recognise the hyperbolic cosine
steps.text: The Taylor series of cosh is exactly the sum of even-power terms of the exponential:

steps.title: Substitute and simplify
steps.text: Replace the series with cosh(λ) and simplify:

key_note: The trick is identifying the even-indexed partial sum of the exponential series as cosh(λ). As λ → ∞, this probability approaches 1/2, which makes intuitive sense.
exam_tip: Whenever a Poisson probability sums over only even or only odd indices, split e^λ and e^{-λ} using cosh and sinh — this reduces the series immediately.
